items.title: New Research Cycle Report Published in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest research cycle report for May 2, 2026, has been published, detailing the review of 17 candidates. All candidates were skipped due to various red flags, with a focus on liquidity concerns. The report also highlights the health of data sources and notes issues with pump.fun firehose access.

items.title: Enable Image and File Uploads for More Models in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update allows image and file uploads for the Claude, Gemini, and Grok-4 models by setting the appropriate capability flags. Previously, only the GPT-5.4 family had these flags enabled, leaving other models with a disabled upload button. Now, users can seamlessly attach images and files, enhancing the multimodal capabilities of these models.

items.title: Enhance Tool Permissions and Actions in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update adds missing English translations for tool permissions and enhances the UI by replacing static icons with interactive buttons for allow, ask, and deny actions. The changes improve user experience by making permissions more intuitive and persistent across sessions. Additionally, the update includes localization for new action tooltips and ensures that the interface aligns with design expectations.

items.title: Enhance Custom MCP Icons with Direct Favicon Links in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the issue where custom MCP connections displayed a generic globe icon instead of the actual server favicon. The solution involves using the server's favicon URL directly for `custom_oauth` connections, ensuring that the correct icon is shown. Tests have been added to verify the functionality and ensure robust handling of various input scenarios.

items.title: Enhance tool-result engagement in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new generic directive in the system prompt to ensure that tool results are treated as authoritative input. It addresses two key issues in aichat2: async tasks stalling and search results not being synthesized. The change is designed to improve the model's interaction with various tools and enhance user experience.

items.title: Enhance Chat Toolbar with External Link Icons in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update simplifies the tooltip text for the Skills and Connections buttons in the chat composer, removing the URL for a cleaner look. Additionally, a new external-link icon has been added next to each button, aligning with the design used in AuthFrontend's sidebar. These changes improve user experience by providing a consistent visual cue for external links.

items.title: Unify Connections Pane Design for a Sleeker Look in Frontend Web ✨
items.summary: This update enhances the visual consistency of the connections page by unifying the background color of the list and detail panes to white. Key changes include a refined active state for list items, improved search input styling, and adjustments to padding and widths for better layout. Overall, these changes aim to create a more polished and user-friendly interface.

items.title: Enable Connections and Skills by Default in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the feature flag gating for the `/user/connections` and `/user/skills` menu entries, allowing them to be enabled by default. The underlying backends have been fully integrated, and the stale `isFeatureEnabled` gate has been removed. The flag system remains in place for future feature rollouts.

items.title: Enhance MCP Tools Display for Connections in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update transforms the connections page by displaying live MCP tools instead of a single access row, significantly improving user experience. Key changes include new types for connection tools, lazy-fetching capabilities, and enhanced UI components to represent each tool with its respective permissions. The backend has also been updated to support fetching these tools from the MCP server.

items.title: Enhance MCP Tool Exposure for Custom OAuth Connections in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new endpoint to retrieve the MCP server's tools list for custom OAuth connections, allowing for a detailed display of available tools. The implementation includes a refresh mechanism for access tokens and supports both JSON and SSE response formats. Additionally, three new unit tests have been added to ensure the functionality works as intended.

items.title: Introducing the DeepSeek V4 Flash Model in Backend 🚀
items.summary: We've added the new DeepSeek V4 Flash model, enhancing cost rules, OpenAPI specifications, and service display pricing. Key updates include new pricing multipliers and visibility adjustments for the model across various services. This update aims to improve API billing and service interactions significantly.

items.title: New deepseek-v4-flash model registered in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: The `deepseek-v4-flash` model has been successfully registered in the OpenAI worker checker configurations. This allows live traffic to effectively route to upstream providers, maintaining the existing rank patterns for providers. The ephone remains the highest-rank fallback provider, ensuring consistency across the deepseek family.

items.title: Enhance Chat Composer with Inline Skills and Connectors in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the previous upload button in the chat composer with three new inline pill buttons: 'Add files or photos', 'Skills', and 'Connectors'. The changes include a new button style and updated localization keys for both English and Chinese. These enhancements aim to improve user experience by providing quick access to essential features directly within the chat interface.

items.title: Improve sidebar logo display in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update fixes the sidebar logo rendering issue by ensuring that the collapsed navigator uses the favicon instead of the full site logo. The `collapsed` prop is now correctly passed to the logo component, enhancing the visual consistency of the interface. The full-width header remains unchanged, continuing to display the wordmark.

items.title: Enhance CI Compatibility in Web Extrator 🚀
items.summary: Updated the deploy script to ensure compatibility with sh by changing the shebang to /bin/sh. This modification allows the script to run correctly under dash on Ubuntu, improving reliability during deployment. The script now uses 'set -eu' for error handling.

items.title: Fix Drive Search Quoting for Google Workspace Skills in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the quoting syntax for Drive search terms in the Google Workspace skills documentation. The PR replaces the incorrect single-quoted JSON wrapper with the proper single-quoted format around the JSON and double quotes around the values. Additionally, it introduces a multi-term recipe and a heredoc fallback for terms containing apostrophes, ensuring better functionality and clarity in usage.

items.title: Automatic i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This PR introduces automatic translation updates for the i18n files based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications include the addition of new language files and updates to existing ones for Arabic, German, and Greek, enhancing the multilingual support of the application. These changes will improve user experience for non-English speakers.

items.title: Fix CI permissions for package deployment in Web Extrator 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the deployment failures on the main branch caused by permission issues with the GITHUB_TOKEN. By adding explicit permissions for packages: write in the deploy job, we ensure successful pushes to the GitHub Container Registry. This change enhances the reliability of our CI/CD pipeline.

items.title: Revamped Skills Page with Claude-style Layout in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The `/user/skills` page has been redesigned to feature a two-column layout, enhancing user experience by displaying the skill list on the left and detailed previews on the right. Key updates include an inline expandable skill view, new dialogs for uploading and writing skills, and improved internationalization support. This update streamlines navigation and interaction, making skill management more intuitive.

items.title: Streamline Provider Data with Server-Driven Approach in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes hard-coded provider mappings from the connector page, allowing the client to render provider data directly from the AuthBackend. Key fields like name, icon_url, and description are now dynamically fetched, enhancing flexibility and maintainability. This change pairs with backend updates to ensure seamless integration.

items.title: Enhance Connector Metadata for Dynamic Provider Info in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces server-rendered fields for connection metadata, including name, icon URL, and description, allowing the AuthFrontend to eliminate hard-coded provider tables. Key changes include new attributes in the OAuthProviderAdapter and updates to built-in adapters to populate these fields. The ProviderCatalogSerializer and ConnectionSerializer now expose the new metadata, streamlining the process of adding new connectors without requiring client releases.

items.title: Enhance Service Creation Process in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes several bugs related to service and API fields, ensuring that the correct attributes are used for display and metadata. New steps have been added to streamline the service creation process, including explicit instructions for uploading service icons and creating document trees. Overall, the skill now includes 26 steps across 10 phases, improving clarity and usability for developers.

items.title: Enhance WebExtrator Documentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update completes the WebExtrator service registration by adding comprehensive documentation, including integration guides and service introductions. Key additions include markdown files for various API endpoints and translations for both English and Chinese. This enhancement aims to improve user understanding and ease of integration with the WebExtrator service.

items.title: Revamp CLI Documentation for Enhanced Accuracy in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update includes a comprehensive rewrite of the SKILL.md files for Google Workspace, GitHub, GitLab, Linear, Notion, and Slack, ensuring all commands align with verified CLI syntax. Key improvements include streamlined command usage, clearer documentation on parameters, and the introduction of sanity checks for authentication. These changes enhance the user experience by reducing errors and improving command efficiency.

items.title: Enhance Kling API Documentation with 4K Mode in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the Kling Videos Generation API documentation in Chinese by adding details about the new `mode=4k` option. This mode is specifically for kling-v3 and kling-v3-omni, and it replaces the previous options of `std` and `pro`. The changes ensure that users are informed about the compatibility and usage of the new 4K feature.

items.title: Launch Independent Nexior Stack for Studio! 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a separate Kubernetes stack for `studio.acedata.cloud`, allowing it to serve the Nexior bundle independently from `hub.acedata.cloud`. Key changes include new deployment, service, and ingress configurations, along with host-aware SEO adjustments to ensure proper canonicalization and routing. This enhancement improves accessibility and performance for users accessing Nexior through the studio domain.

items.title: Enhance Google Workspace Skill Documentation in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update rewrites the Google Workspace skill documentation to clarify the correct usage of path parameters and available helper commands. Key changes include a new section on calling methods, explicit examples for path parameters, and the introduction of new recipes for common tasks. These improvements aim to streamline user interactions with the Google Workspace API and reduce errors in command invocations.

items.title: Enhance image2video submission validation in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the image2video submission process by adding validation to ensure at least one reference image is uploaded. If no images are provided, a clear warning message will be displayed to the user instead of a generic error. Additionally, empty image URLs will be stripped from the payload to prevent issues with future requests.

items.title: Revamped Connectors Page with Claude-Style Layout in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The Connectors page has been redesigned to feature a Claude-style two-pane layout. The left pane includes a search input and a collapsible list of connected and available connectors, while the right pane displays detailed information for each connector. This update enhances user experience and maintains existing dialog functionalities.

items.title: Launch WebExtrator Service with APIs and Cost Rules in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the new WebExtrator service to the AceDataCloud platform, enabling high-concurrency web page rendering and intelligent content extraction. Key features include three new API endpoints for rendering, extracting, and task management, along with associated cost rules and OpenAPI specifications. The service is now fully integrated and ready for use, with all tests passing successfully.

items.title: Make .env file optional for CI in Web Extrator 🚀
items.summary: This update marks the `.env` file reference in the Docker Compose configuration as optional. This change resolves the CI failure during the `docker compose push` step, allowing for smoother deployments without requiring a local `.env` file. Production environment variables will continue to be managed through Kubernetes manifests.

items.title: Exciting Launch of Native 4K Mode for Kling in Backend 🎉
items.summary: The new native 4K mode has been officially added to Kling, specifically for the kling-v3 and kling-v3-omni models. Key updates include modifications to the OpenAPI to support the new mode, updated API and service cost rules, and the addition of comprehensive tests to ensure functionality. This enhancement allows users to generate high-quality 4K videos at a competitive rate of $0.42 per second.

items.title: Unlocking Native 4K Support for Kling in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces support for native 4K mode in Kling, specifically for the kling-v3 and kling-v3-omni models. Key changes include validation for 4K mode usage and updated pricing entries, ensuring a seamless experience for users. The pricing for 4K is set at $0.42 per second, with no audio surcharge applicable.

items.title: sanitize MCP server names for OpenAI compatibility in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical bug where invalid MCP server names could disrupt tool calling in OpenAI. The new `slugifyServerName` function ensures that server names are sanitized to meet OpenAI's regex requirements, preventing issues for users with problematic MCP connections. Comprehensive tests have been added to verify the functionality, ensuring all existing tests pass successfully.

items.title: Enhance model skill usage guidance in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the model's understanding of the `<skills>` block by clarifying that these are playbooks, not tools. The `DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` has been updated to accurately reflect available tools and how to invoke skills. Additionally, a preamble has been added to the `<skills>` block to prevent confusion during skill activation.

items.title: Enhance CI for Auto-Translate PRs in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the issue where auto-translate PRs were stuck in a BLOCKED state due to the use of the default GITHUB_TOKEN. By switching to the BOT_GITHUB_TOKEN for actions like checkout and create-pull-request, we ensure that these PRs will now trigger the necessary CI checks. This change will improve the workflow for future auto-translate runs.

items.title: Fix skill catalog bundling issue in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where the SKILL.md files were not included in the runtime image, causing the 'google-workspace' skill to be unavailable. The Dockerfile has been modified to copy the necessary skill catalog from the builder stage, ensuring that the skills loader can access the required files. Post-deployment verification will confirm that the skills are now correctly recognized and functional.

items.title: Enhance Skills UX with Tabs and Inline Editing in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses four key UX issues on the `/user/skills` page, improving user interaction significantly. Tabs have replaced icon toggles for a cleaner interface, and inline editing has been introduced for `SKILL.md`, allowing users to edit directly within the preview. Additionally, missing icons have been registered to enhance visual consistency across the application.

items.title: Streamline SKILL.md Handling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves issues with double-storing SKILL.md and fixes 404 errors for markdown-only skills. The validation process has been enhanced to ensure that SKILL.md is consistently included in the asset bundle manifest, improving user experience and data integrity. Additionally, existing rows have been backfilled to maintain consistency across the database.

items.title: Exciting New Routes for AceDataCloud Integration in Web Extrator 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new platform-compatible routes for the WebExtrator, including `POST /webextrator/render`, `POST /webextrator/extract`, and `POST /webextrator/tasks`. These routes follow the AceDataCloud upstream worker contract, enabling both synchronous and asynchronous processing with a standardized response format. Additionally, tasks are now persisted in Redis, enhancing reliability and scalability.

items.title: Enhance Midjourney Image Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses issues with `sub_image_urls` from the Midjourney API, ensuring they are routed through the platform CDN instead of the Discord CDN. The fix includes deriving image URLs from COS-hosted images and correcting the reported dimensions to match the actual grid size. This change improves accessibility and consistency for users relying on these images.

items.title: Fix skill button to link directly to skills management in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update resolves a bug where the skills dialog failed to load due to a deleted endpoint. The skill button now opens a direct link to the skills management page at auth.acedata.cloud/user/skills. Several unused components and code related to the previous skills dialog have been removed, streamlining the chat interface.

items.title: Enhancements to Analytics and Pool Selection in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces key improvements including a new analytics filter to exclude historical `buy` actions below the minimum score, enhancing the accuracy of aggregated statistics. Additionally, LLM prompt hardening has been implemented to enforce new liquidity rules, preventing erroneous `buy` actions. Finally, the base-token pool selection logic has been refined to eliminate null grader rows by ensuring only relevant pools are considered.

items.title: Enhance Strategy: Block Thin-LP Rugs & Enforce Minimum Buy Score in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces critical enhancements to the strategy by implementing a new flag to block thin-LP rugs and enforcing a minimum buy score for shadow buys. The changes include a scoring penalty for insufficient LP lock and a new CLI option to demote below-threshold buy actions. These improvements aim to enhance model quality and ensure safer trading practices.

items.title: Fix production token issues for aichat2 in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical production blocker by adding the `CONNECTION_INTERNAL_TOKEN` and `AUTH_BACKEND_URL` to the aichat2 deployment configuration. These changes ensure that the aichat2 worker can successfully authenticate with the AuthBackend, preventing silent failures and credential issues for signed-in users. Additionally, unnecessary environment variables have been removed to streamline the deployment process.

items.title: Enhance MCP Integration with Auto-Promoted Virtual Skills in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new feature that allows prompts from active MCP connections to be treated as virtual skills, enhancing the integration with the model. Key changes include the implementation of a new MCPClientManager method for listing prompts, adjustments to the conversations handler for merging skills, and updates to the requestSkills flow. Additionally, new tests have been added to ensure the functionality works as expected.

items.title: Fix radio binding and ensure SKILL.md visibility in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update resolves two key issues on the `/user/skills` page. The radio button for selecting markdown now binds correctly, and SKILL.md will always be displayed in the file tree, even for markdown-only skills. Additionally, the sidebar icon for skills has been registered to eliminate the blank square issue.

items.title: Seamless Skill Integration for Conversations in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update implements the core functionality for skill integration within the conversation flow, allowing the model to utilize skills end-to-end. Key changes include the introduction of a per-conversation flow for managing active skills and the ability to inject OAuth tokens dynamically. Additionally, new asset shipping capabilities have been added to enhance the interaction with external tools.

items.title: Enhanced Skills UI with File Tree and Previews in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a new file tree and per-file preview feature in the skills detail drawer, significantly improving the user experience on the `/user/skills` page. The drawer now displays skill metadata alongside a file browser, allowing users to easily navigate and preview files with various renderers for markdown, HTML, images, and code. Additionally, security measures have been implemented to ensure safe rendering of content.

items.title: Enhance File Fetching for Skill Previews in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new endpoint `GET /api/v1/skills/<id>/files/?path=<rel-path>` for fetching individual files from a skill's asset bundle, enhancing the preview UI. Key changes include improved content serving, a caching mechanism for efficiency, and comprehensive tests to ensure functionality and security. The new features will support a more dynamic user experience in the upcoming frontend integration.

items.title: Feat(skills): accept .zip + .tar archives in upload form in Frontend Web ✨
Fix(skills): accept .zip + .tar archives, strip ./ prefix, auto-find SKILL.md in Backend 🐛
Feat(aichat2): SkillLoader + asset cache + load_skill tool in Platform Service ✨
Ci: auto-translate i18n from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🧪
Feat(skills): add /user/skills page (Mode A markdown + Mode B tarball) in Frontend Web ✨
